warning: AI-assisted monitoring. Verify against official legislation before product approval, export, label declaration, or customer compliance confirmation.

rules.sourceTitle: Food Additives DB (GSFA Online) | CODEXALIMENTARIUS FAO-WHO
rules.notes: The Codex General Standard for Food Additives (GSFA, Codex STAN 192-1995) online database provides searchable provisions for food additives, functional classes, and food categories. Users are encouraged to consult the Preamble of the GSFA for interpretation.
rules.evidenceQuote: The "Codex General Standard for Food Additives" (GSFA, Codex STAN 192-1995) sets forth the conditions under which permitted food additives may be used in all foods...

rules.notes: The Codex Alimentarius Commission establishes maximum levels (MLs) and guideline levels for contaminants and naturally occurring toxicants in food and feed, including mycotoxins and metals. These are detailed in the General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995) and related Codes of Practice.
rules.evidenceQuote: The Codex maximum level (ML) for a contaminant in a food or feed commodity is the maximum concentration of that substance recommended by the Codex Alimentarius Commission to be legally permitted in that commodity.

rules.notes: The database provides information on approved food additives and their conditions of use, based on Annex II of Regulation (EC) No 1333/2008.
rules.evidenceQuote: This database can serve as a tool to inform about the food additives approved for use in food in the EU and their conditions of use. It is based on the Union list of food additives. This list is in Annex II of Regulation (EC) No 1333/2008.

rules.parameter: Foodborne bacteria (e.g., Salmonella, Listeria monocytogenes, Enterobacter sakazakii), their toxins and metabolites (e.g., staphylococcal enterotoxins, histamine)
rules.sourceTitle: Microbiological criteria - Food Safety - European Commission
rules.notes: Commission Regulation (EC) No 2073/2005 lays down food safety criteria for relevant foodborne bacteria, their toxins and metabolites, and process hygiene criteria for foods.
rules.evidenceQuote: Commission Regulation (EC) No 2073/2005 on microbiological criteria for foods, applicable from 1 January 2006, lays down food safety criteria for relevant foodborne bacteria, their toxins and metabolites, such as Salmonella , Listeria monocytogenes , Enterobacter sakazakii , staphylococcal enterotoxins and histamine in specific foods.

rules.parameter: General labelling requirements for prepackaged foodstuffs
rules.sourceTitle: GSO 9:2022 لائحة فنية خليجية --> بطاقات المواد الغذائية المعبأة
rules.notes: This technical regulation specifies the mandatory labelling requirements for all prepackaged food products in the GCC region.
rules.evidenceQuote: بطاقات المواد الغذائية المعبأة

rules.sourceTitle: GSO 2055-1:2015 لائحة فنية خليجية --> الأغذية الحلال – الجزء الأول : الاشتراطات العامة للأغذية الحلال
rules.notes: This technical regulation outlines the general requirements for food products to be considered Halal, impacting Halal claims and labelling.
rules.evidenceQuote: الاشتراطات العامة للأغذية الحلال

rules.parameter: Mandatory shelf life periods for food products
rules.sourceTitle: GSO 150-1:2013 لائحة فنية خليجية --> فترات صلاحية المنتجات الغذائية - الجزء الأول : فترات الصلاحية الإلزامية
rules.notes: This technical regulation specifies the mandatory shelf life periods that must be declared on food product labels.
rules.evidenceQuote: فترات الصلاحية الإلزامية

rules.parameter: Optional shelf life periods for food products
rules.sourceTitle: GSO 150-2:2013 مواصفة قياسية خليجية --> فترات صلاحية المنتجات الغذائية - الجزء الثاني : فترات الصلاحية الاختيارية
rules.notes: This standard provides guidelines for optional shelf life periods for food products, which may be declared on labels.
rules.evidenceQuote: فترات الصلاحية الاختيارية

rules.parameter: General labelling requirements for prepackaged food
rules.notes: Specific labelling requirements for prepackaged food products must be verified against GSO 9:2022.
rules.evidenceQuote: GSO 9:2022 Gulf Technical Regulation --> Labeling of prepackaged food stuffs

rules.foodCategory: Foodstuffs intended for human consumption, food ingredients (including processed foods, seasoning, snack food)
rules.notes: Specific microbiological limits for foodstuffs and food ingredients must be verified against GSO 1016:2015.
rules.evidenceQuote: GSO 1016:2015 Gulf Technical Regulation ... Microbiological Criteria for Foodstuffs ... This GSO technical regulation is concerned with microbiological limits for some foodstuffs intended for human consumption and for some food ingredients used in food industry.

rules.limit: Verification required as per FSSAI Food Additives Regulations
rules.notes: Specific additive limits for seasoning, snack food, and ready-to-eat snacks must be verified against the FSSAI Food Additives Regulations.
rules.evidenceQuote: Compendium_Food_Additives_Regulations_20_12_2022.pdf

rules.limit: Verification required as per FSSAI Labelling and Display Regulations
rules.sourceTitle: Compendium of Labelling and Display Regulations
rules.notes: Mandatory allergen declarations for seasoning, snack food, and ready-to-eat snacks must be verified against the FSSAI Labelling and Display Regulations.
rules.evidenceQuote: Compendium_Labelling_Display_30_06_2022.pdf

rules.limit: Verification required as per FSSAI Labelling and Display Regulations
rules.sourceTitle: Compendium of Labelling and Display Regulations
rules.notes: All food products, including seasoning, snack food, and ready-to-eat snacks, must comply with general labelling requirements.
rules.evidenceQuote: Compendium_Labelling_Display_30_06_2022.pdf

rules.sourceTitle: Surat Edaran Nomor 2 Tahun 2026 tentang Ketentuan Produksi, Importasi, Registrasi, dan Peredaran Bahan Tambahan Pangan Dinitrogen Monoksida (N2O)
rules.notes: This circular letter outlines the provisions for the production, import, registration, and circulation of Dinitrogen Monoxide (N2O) as a food additive. Specific limits and conditions must be verified.
rules.evidenceQuote: SE Nomor 2 Tahun 2026 Surat Edaran Nomor 2 Tahun 2026 tentang Ketentuan Produksi, Importasi, Registrasi, dan Peredaran Bahan Tambahan Pangan Dinitrogen Monoksida (N2O)

rules.parameter: Compliance with regulations for processed foods
rules.sourceTitle: Jaringan Dokumentasi dan Informasi Hukum Badan Pengawas Obat dan Makanan RI
rules.notes: The Indonesian National Agency of Drug and Food Control (BPOM) maintains 281 regulations specifically for processed foods, which include seasoning and snack products. Specific limits and requirements must be verified by consulting these regulations.
rules.evidenceQuote: 281 Peraturan Pangan Olahan

rules.sourceTitle: PERKABPOM Nomor 34 Tahun 2019 Tentang Kategori Pangan
rules.notes: This regulation defines the categories for processed foods, which is crucial for determining applicable specific regulations regarding additives, contaminants, and labelling.
rules.evidenceQuote: PERKABPOM Nomor 34 Tahun 2019 Tentang Kategori Pangan

rules.limit: Additives (excluding natural flavoring agents and generally consumed substances used as additives) and preparations/food containing additives are prohibited unless specified by MHLW as unlikely to cause harm. Verification of MHLW specifications is required.
rules.notes: This establishes a positive list system for food additives.
rules.evidenceQuote: Additives (excluding natural flavoring agents and things that have generally been served for human consumption, and are used as additives) and preparations and food containing additives must not be sold... except for cases that the Minister of Health, Labour and Welfare specifies as unlikely to cause harm to human health...

rules.limit: Food or additives which are likely to cause harm to human health due to contamination or the addition of foreign substances or on other grounds must not be sold or handled for sale.
rules.evidenceQuote: The following food and additives must not be sold... (iv) those which are likely to cause harm to human health due to contamination or the addition of foreign substances or on other grounds.

rules.parameter: Residues of agricultural chemicals, feed additives, veterinary drugs
rules.limit: Food containing residues of agricultural chemicals, feed additives, or pharmaceutical products for animals exceeding MHLW specified quantities must not be sold or handled for sale. Verification of MHLW specified quantities is required.
rules.notes: This refers to the 'Positive List System for Agricultural Chemical Residues in Food'.
rules.evidenceQuote: Food in which substances that are the ingredients of agricultural chemicals... feed... and pharmaceutical products... remain in a quantity exceeding the quantity that the Minister of Health, Labour and Welfare specifies as unlikely to cause harm to human health... must not be produced, imported... or sold...

rules.limit: Food or additives which contain or bear toxic or harmful substances or are suspected to contain or bear such substances must not be sold or handled for sale, unless specified by MHLW as unlikely to cause harm.
rules.evidenceQuote: The following food and additives must not be sold... (ii) those which contain or bear toxic or harmful substances or are suspected to contain or bear such substances; provided, however, that this does not apply when the Minister of Health, Labour and Welfare specifies that the substances are unlikely to cause harm to human health;

rules.parameter: Compliance with MHLW established standards and criteria
rules.limit: Food or additives must not be produced, processed, used, cooked, or preserved using methods that do not conform to MHLW established criteria, and food or additives that do not conform to MHLW established standards must not be produced, imported, processed, used, cooked, preserved, or sold. Verification of specific standards and criteria is required.
rules.notes: MHLW has the authority to establish these standards and criteria.
rules.evidenceQuote: When the criteria or standards have been established pursuant to the provisions of the preceding paragraph, food or additives must not be produced, processed, used, cooked, or preserved using methods that do not conform to such criteria; food or additives that do not conform to such criteria must not be sold or imported; and food or additives that do not conform to such standards must not be produced, imported, processed, used, cooked, preserved, or sold.

rules.limit: Food business operators must endeavor to ensure the safety of food, additives, apparatus, or containers and packaging, obtain necessary knowledge and skills, ensure raw material safety, conduct voluntary inspections, and take other necessary measures.
rules.notes: General responsibility for food business operators.
rules.evidenceQuote: A food business operator... must endeavor to ensure the safety of the food, additives, apparatus, or containers and packaging which they collect, produce, import, process, cook, store, transport, sell, provide to many and unspecified persons, or use in business...

rules.limit: Meat, milk, organs of livestock, and meat, organs of poultry, and specified products thereof must not be imported for sale without certificates from the exporting country's governmental organization, certifying they are free from specified diseases or abnormalities.
rules.notes: Applies to animal products.
rules.evidenceQuote: The meat, milk and organs of livestock... must not be imported as food for the purpose of sale, unless they have certificates or their copies attached which have been issued by a governmental organization of an exporting country, and which certify that they are not the meat, milk or organs of livestock... which have or are suspected to have any of the diseases listed in each item of the preceding paragraph...

rules.limit: Food and additives specified by MHLW as requiring special process control measures must not be imported for sale unless produced or processed in MHLW-specified countries, regions, or facilities that actually take those measures. Verification of MHLW specifications is required.
rules.evidenceQuote: The food and additives specified by Ministry of Health, Labour and Welfare Order as requiring measures to control particularly important processes... must not be imported for the purpose of sale, unless they have been produced or processed in the countries or regions, or facilities specified by the Minister of Health, Labour and Welfare as actually taking those measures.

rules.parameter: Import certification for production site sanitation management
rules.limit: Food and additives specified by MHLW as requiring certification of production site food sanitation management must not be imported for sale unless accompanied by certificates from the exporting country's governmental organization. Verification of MHLW specifications is required.
rules.evidenceQuote: The food and additives specified by Ministry of Health, Labour and Welfare Order as requiring certification of the conditions of food sanitation management at the production site... must not be imported for the purpose of sale, unless they have certificates or copies thereof attached which have been issued by a governmental organization of an exporting country and which contain the relevant matters.

rules.parameter: Notification of harm from designated ingredients
rules.limit: A business handling food containing a 'designated ingredient, etc.' must notify the relevant prefectural governor, etc. without delay upon obtaining information that the food causes or may cause harm to human health. Verification of MHLW designated ingredients is required.
rules.notes: This is a post-market surveillance/reporting requirement.
rules.evidenceQuote: A business that handles food containing any ingredient or material which requires special attention from the viewpoint of preventing food sanitation hazards and which has been designated by the Minister of Health, Labour and Welfare... upon obtaining information that any food containing designated ingredient, etc. handled by the business causes or may cause harm to human health, must notify the relevant prefectural governor, etc. of that information without delay...

rules.limit: Food business operators must endeavor to make a record of necessary information (e.g., seller's name) and retain it, to prevent food sanitation hazards.
rules.evidenceQuote: A food business operator must endeavor to make a record of any necessary information such as the name of the person who has sold food for sale, etc. or raw materials thereof to the food business operator and retain the record...

rules.limit: Verification of specific standards and specifications for food, food additives, apparatus, and containers and packaging, including limits for various parameters, is required.
rules.sourceTitle: Specifications and Standards for Food, Food Additives, etc. Under The Food Sanitation Act
rules.notes: This document provides the detailed limits and standards referenced by the Food Sanitation Act.
rules.evidenceQuote: Specifications and Standards for Food, Food Additives, etc. Under The Food Sanitation Act

rules.limit: Food or additives must be collected, produced, processed, used, cooked, stored, transported, displayed and delivered in a clean and sanitary manner.
rules.notes: Applies to all food and additives meant for sale.
rules.evidenceQuote: Food or additives which are meant to be sold... must be collected, produced, processed, used, cooked, stored, transported, displayed and delivered in a clean and sanitary manner.

rules.limit: Food or additives which are contaminated with pathogens or are suspected to be so, and which are likely to cause harm to human health, must not be sold or handled for sale.
rules.evidenceQuote: The following food and additives must not be sold... (iii) those which are contaminated with pathogens or are suspected to be so, and which are likely to cause harm to human health;

rules.limit: Food or additives which are rotten or have deteriorated, or are immature, must not be sold or handled for sale, unless unlikely to cause harm and recognized as edible.
rules.evidenceQuote: The following food and additives must not be sold... (i) those which are rotten or have deteriorated, or are immature; provided, however, that this does not apply to those which are unlikely to cause harm to human health in general and are recognized as edible;

rules.limit: Must be assessed for safety by SFA
rules.notes: This applies to all food products, including seasoning powder, seasoning sauce, snack seasoning, and ready-to-eat snacks.
rules.evidenceQuote: Food additives must be assessed for safety by SFA before they can be used in food sold in Singapore.

rules.notes: Verification is required using the SFA's List of Permitted Food Additives or Food Additives Search tool. This applies to all food products, including seasoning powder, seasoning sauce, snack seasoning, and ready-to-eat snacks.
rules.evidenceQuote: Only food additives that have been assessed by SFA are permitted for use in food products sold in Singapore.

rules.limit: Must have technological justification and not present health risks
rules.notes: This applies to all food products, including seasoning powder, seasoning sauce, snack seasoning, and ready-to-eat snacks.
rules.evidenceQuote: Food additives are allowed when: there is a technological justification they do not present health risks to consumers

rules.limit: Verification required for specific limits based on additive and food type
rules.notes: Use the Guidance Information on Requirements for Use of Food Additives, List of Permitted Food Additives, or Food Additives Search tool. This applies to all food products, including seasoning powder, seasoning sauce, snack seasoning, and ready-to-eat snacks.
rules.evidenceQuote: Different limits apply, depending on the additive and the type of food products. Use the Guidance Information on Requirements for Use of Food Additives to learn more about the different types of additives and the corresponding regulations that impose the limits.

rules.limit: All food businesses must comply with regulatory limits
rules.notes: This applies to all food products, including seasoning powder, seasoning sauce, snack seasoning, and ready-to-eat snacks.
rules.evidenceQuote: All food businesses offering food for sale in Singapore must comply with the regulatory limits on food additives.

rules.limit: Do not import or sell if additive is not permitted
rules.notes: This applies to all food products, including seasoning powder, seasoning sauce, snack seasoning, and ready-to-eat snacks.
rules.evidenceQuote: If your food product contains an additive that is not permitted, do not import or sell it.

rules.limit: Businesses must be aware of common contaminants and conduct laboratory tests as needed
rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.notes: This applies to all food products, including seasoning powder, seasoning sauce, snack seasoning, and ready-to-eat snacks.
rules.evidenceQuote: Food businesses must be aware of the contaminants that are commonly found in their food products and conduct laboratory tests as needed.

rules.limit: Must be within SFA’s regulatory limits
rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.notes: This applies to all food products, including seasoning powder, seasoning sauce, snack seasoning, and ready-to-eat snacks.
rules.evidenceQuote: Contaminants (also known as incidental constituents) in food sold in Singapore must be within SFA’s regulatory limits.

rules.limit: All food businesses must comply with regulatory limits
rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.notes: This applies to all food products, including seasoning powder, seasoning sauce, snack seasoning, and ready-to-eat snacks.
rules.evidenceQuote: All food businesses must comply with the regulatory limits on incidental constituents i.e. contaminants in food.

rules.limit: Do not import or sell if contaminants exceed regulatory limits
rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.notes: This applies to all food products, including seasoning powder, seasoning sauce, snack seasoning, and ready-to-eat snacks.
rules.evidenceQuote: If your food or food products contain contaminants or incidental constituents that exceed the regulatory limits, do not import or sell them.

rules.foodCategory: Meat and food derived from meat
rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.notes: This applies if seasoning or snack products contain meat or meat derivatives.
rules.evidenceQuote: Regulation 33 of the Food Regulations prohibits the import or sale of meat and any food derived from meat which contains residues of the following compounds: diethylstibestrol (3, 4-bis(p-hydroxyphenyl)-3-hexene) hexoestrol (3, 4-bis(p-hydroxyphenyl)-n-hexane) dienoestrol (3, 4-bis(p-hydroxyphenyl)-2, 4-hexadiene)

rules.limit: Verification required for Maximum Residue Limits (MRLs)
rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.notes: MRLs are established for various food commodities. Refer to the SFA website for the list. This applies if seasoning or snack products contain ingredients from commodities with established MRLs.
rules.evidenceQuote: SFA has also assessed the risk of the presence of other veterinary drug residues in various food commodities, and has established a list of maximum residue limits (MRLs) for veterinary drug residues for compliance by food businesses with effect from 1 May 2020.

rules.parameter: Various contaminants (e.g., heavy metals, mycotoxins, pesticide residues)
rules.limit: Verification required for specific limits based on contaminant type
rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.notes: Refer to relevant SFA documents for details on limits for heavy metals, mycotoxins, marine biotoxins, veterinary drug residues, pesticide residues, and other incidental constituents. This applies to all food products, including seasoning powder, seasoning sauce, snack seasoning, and ready-to-eat snacks.
rules.evidenceQuote: There are various limits imposed on different types of contaminants or incidental constituents in food. For more details, please refer to the relevant documents below: Heavy metals, Mycotoxins and Marine biotoxins, Veterinary drug residues, Pesticide residues, Other incidental constituents.

rules.limit: All ingredients and additives must be declared on food labels
rules.notes: This applies to prepacked seasoning powder, seasoning sauce, snack seasoning, and ready-to-eat snacks.
rules.evidenceQuote: All ingredients and additives used in food products must be declared in list of ingredients on food labels.

rules.limit: Verification required for specific microbiological standards
rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.notes: Refer to relevant SFA documents for details on microbiological standards for ready-to-eat food and non-ready-to-eat food. This applies to ready-to-eat snacks, and ingredients for seasoning/snacks.
rules.evidenceQuote: Microbiological standards Ready-to-eat food Non-ready-to-eat food (till 3 Mar 2024) Non-ready-to-eat food (from 4 Mar 2024)

rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.notes: This applies to ready-to-eat snacks.
rules.evidenceQuote: Ready-to-eat (RTE) food are not required to be tested for total plate count (TPC).

rules.sourceTitle: Use Restrictions and Labeling Requirements of γ-Aminobutyric Acid Produced by Microbial Fermentation as a Food Ingredient
rules.notes: Specific use restrictions for γ-Aminobutyric Acid (GABA) as a food ingredient must be verified against the official regulation.
rules.evidenceQuote: Use Restrictions and Labeling Requirements of γ-Aminobutyric Acid Produced by Microbial Fermentation as a Food Ingredient

rules.limit: Crustacean shellfish, mango, peanuts, sesame seeds, milk and goat milk, eggs, tree nuts, gluten-containing cereals, soybeans, fish, and sulphite (as residue sulphur dioxide)
rules.sourceTitle: Regulations Governing Food Allergen Labeling on the Recommended Labeling Allergens
rules.notes: These 11 allergens must be declared on prepackaged food labels if present as an ingredient or food additive.
rules.evidenceQuote: food allergen labeling is required for the products that contain any of the following 11 allergens as an ingredient or food additive: Crustacean shellfish, mango, peanuts, sesame seeds, milk and goat milk, eggs, tree nuts, gluten-containing cereals, soybeans, fish, and sulphite as the residue sulphur dioxide for 10 or more milligrams per kilogram of food.

rules.limit: Cephalopods and cephalopod products, Spiral shells and Spiral shell products thereof, Seeds and seed products (excluding highly refined sunflower seed oil), Kiwis and kiwi products thereof
rules.sourceTitle: Regulations Governing Food Allergen Labeling on the Recommended Labeling Allergens
rules.notes: Labeling is recommended for these allergens if present as an ingredient or food additive.
rules.evidenceQuote: food allergen labeling is also recommended for the products that contain any of the following allergens as an ingredient or food additive: (1) Cephalopods and cephalopod products... (2) Spiral shells and Spiral shell products thereof... (3) Seeds and seed products... (4) Kiwis and kiwi products thereof...

rules.sourceTitle: Regulations Governing Food Allergen Labeling on the Recommended Labeling Allergens
rules.notes: Sulphite must be declared as an allergen if the residue sulphur dioxide is 10 mg/kg or more.
rules.evidenceQuote: sulphite as the residue sulphur dioxide for 10 or more milligrams per kilogram of food.

rules.sourceTitle: Standards for Pesticide Residue Limits in Foods
rules.notes: Specific limits for pesticide residues in foods must be verified against the official standard.
rules.evidenceQuote: Standards for Pesticide Residue Limits in Foods

rules.sourceTitle: Standards for Pesticide Residue Limits in Animal Products
rules.notes: Specific limits for pesticide residues in animal products (which may be ingredients in processed foods) must be verified against the official standard.
rules.evidenceQuote: Standards for Pesticide Residue Limits in Animal Products

rules.limit: Control measures shall be set in place
rules.sourceTitle: Regulations Governing Food Allergen Labeling on the Recommended Labeling Allergens
rules.notes: Control measures are required to prevent allergen cross-contamination during production.
rules.evidenceQuote: Control measures shall be set in place to prevent food from cross contamination in the production process.

rules.foodCategory: Prepackaged Food Products, Seasoning, Snack Food
rules.sourceTitle: Revised Regulations on Nutrition Claim for Prepackaged Food Products
rules.notes: Specific requirements for nutrition claims on prepackaged food products must be verified against the official regulation.
rules.evidenceQuote: Revised Regulations on Nutrition Claim for Prepackaged Food Products

rules.sourceTitle: Regulations Governing Food Allergen Labeling on the Recommended Labeling Allergens
rules.notes: If there is a risk of allergen cross-contamination, a precautionary statement like 'The plant, equipment or production line used to produce the product is also used to process○○' is suggested.
rules.evidenceQuote: the following statement of precautionary allergen labeling is suggested: “ The plant, equipment or production line used to produce the product is also used to process○○” or the synonymous terms.

rules.sourceTitle: Use Restrictions and Labeling Requirements of γ-Aminobutyric Acid Produced by Microbial Fermentation as a Food Ingredient
rules.notes: Specific labeling requirements for γ-Aminobutyric Acid (GABA) as a food ingredient must be verified against the official regulation.
rules.evidenceQuote: Use Restrictions and Labeling Requirements of γ-Aminobutyric Acid Produced by Microbial Fermentation as a Food Ingredient

rules.sourceTitle: กองอาหาร FOOD DIVISION
rules.notes: Specific permitted food additives and their limits must be verified in relevant Ministry of Public Health Notifications and FDA Notifications.
rules.evidenceQuote: วัตถุเจือปนอาหาร

rules.sourceTitle: กองอาหาร FOOD DIVISION
rules.notes: Verification of permitted and prohibited ingredients for use in food is required from relevant regulations.
rules.evidenceQuote: ส่วนประกอบที่อนุญาต/ไม่อนุญาตให้ใช้ในอาหาร

rules.sourceTitle: กองอาหาร FOOD DIVISION
rules.notes: Specific contaminant limits must be verified in relevant regulations.
rules.evidenceQuote: มาตรฐานอาหารที่มีสารปนเปื้อน

rules.sourceTitle: กองอาหาร FOOD DIVISION
rules.notes: A list of foods prohibited for production, import, or sale exists and must be strictly adhered to. Prohibition may be due to safety concerns (e.g., contaminants, unapproved substances).
rules.evidenceQuote: อาหารที่ห้ามผลิต นำเข้า หรือจำหน่าย

rules.sourceTitle: กองอาหาร FOOD DIVISION
rules.notes: Specific limits for toxins and veterinary drug residues must be verified in relevant regulations.
rules.evidenceQuote: มาตรฐานอาหารที่มีสารพิษ-ยาสัตว์ตกค้าง

rules.sourceTitle: กองอาหาร FOOD DIVISION
rules.notes: Food products must comply with general and specific food labeling and nutrition labeling requirements, which must be verified in relevant regulations.
rules.evidenceQuote: การแสดงฉลากอาหารและฉลากโภชนาการ

rules.sourceTitle: กองอาหาร FOOD DIVISION
rules.notes: Specific microbiological standards for pathogenic microorganisms must be verified in relevant regulations.
rules.evidenceQuote: มาตรฐานอาหารด้านจุลินทรีย์ที่ทำให้เกิดโรค

rules.sourceTitle: Substances Added to Food (formerly EAFUS) | FDA
rules.notes: Delisted color additives in FDA regulations (21 CFR 81.10 and/or 81.30) are prohibited.
rules.evidenceQuote: Delisted color additives in FDA regulations (21 CFR 81.10 and/or 81.30) (labeled as "DELISTED").

rules.notes: Food additives and color additives must be listed in FDA regulations (21 CFR Parts 172, 173, 73, 74) or evaluated by FEMA/JECFA for flavoring substances.
rules.evidenceQuote: Food additives and color additives that are listed in FDA regulations (21 CFR Parts 172, 173 and Parts 73, 74 respectively), and flavoring substances evaluated by FEMA* and JECFA*.

rules.notes: GRAS substances must be listed in FDA regulations (21 CFR Parts 182 and 184) or in the GRAS Notice Inventory.
rules.evidenceQuote: Generally Recognized as Safe ("GRAS") substances that are listed in FDA regulations (21 CFR Parts 182 and 184).

rules.notes: Substances approved for specific uses in foods prior to September 6, 1958, must be listed in 21 CFR Part 181.
rules.evidenceQuote: Substances approved for specific uses in foods prior to September 6, 1958, known as prior-sanctioned substances (21 CFR Part 181).

rules.sourceTitle: Substances Added to Food (formerly EAFUS) | FDA
rules.notes: Substances listed in FDA regulations (21 CFR Part 189) are prohibited from use in food.
rules.evidenceQuote: Prohibited substances that are listed in FDA regulations (21 CFR Part 189) as prohibited from use in food (labeled as "PROHIBITED" or "PROHIBITED WITH EXCEPTIONS").

rules.limit: Milk, eggs, fish, Crustacean shellfish, tree nuts, peanuts, wheat, soybeans, and sesame
rules.notes: The FASTER Act declared sesame as the 9th major food allergen, effective January 1, 2023. All FDA requirements applicable to major food allergens, including labeling and manufacturing requirements, apply to sesame as of this date.
rules.evidenceQuote: The major food allergens are milk, eggs, fish, Crustacean shellfish, tree nuts, peanuts, wheat, soybeans, and sesame.

rules.notes: Guidance Level for 3-Monochloropropane-1,2-Diol (3-MCPD).
rules.evidenceQuote: 3-Monochloropropane-1,2-Diol (3-MCPD) Acid-hydrolyzed protein and Asian-style sauces Guidance Level 1 ppm CPG 500.500

rules.notes: Action Level for total aflatoxins in foods.
rules.evidenceQuote: Aflatoxins, total Foods Action Level 20 ppb CPG 555.400

rules.notes: Contaminant levels (tolerances, action levels, guidance levels, derived intervention levels, recommended maximum levels, and advisory levels) for poisonous or deleterious substances in human food must be verified using the Chemical Contaminants Transparency Tool and 21 CFR parts 109 and 509.
rules.evidenceQuote: The Chemical Contaminants Transparency Tool provides a consolidated list of these levels. Contaminant levels listed in the transparency tool include tolerances, action levels, guidance levels, derived intervention levels, recommended maximum levels, and advisory levels. Tolerances and action levels are defined in 21 Code of Federal Regulations (CFR) parts 109 and 509.

rules.notes: Advisory statements are not required by law and should only be used to address unavoidable cross-contact after good manufacturing practices have been incorporated.
rules.evidenceQuote: Such statements are not required by law and can be used to address unavoidable “cross-contact,” only if manufacturers have incorporated good manufacturing processes in their facility and have taken every precaution to avoid cross-contact

rules.notes: Facilities must implement allergen preventive controls to prevent allergen cross-contact in manufacturing and packaging and to prevent undeclared allergens, as per the CGMP & PC rule (21 CFR part 117).
rules.evidenceQuote: The CGMP & PC rule includes requirements for allergen preventive controls to prevent allergen cross-contact in manufacturing and packaging and to prevent undeclared allergens.

rules.notes: Color additives made from cochineal extract and carmine must be declared on the label of all food and cosmetic products.
rules.evidenceQuote: Color additives made from cochineal extract and carmine, which are derived from insects, have been identified as allergenic substances that must be declared on the label of all food and cosmetic products.

rules.notes: All products containing FD&C Yellow No. 5 must identify it on their labels.
rules.evidenceQuote: The FDA requires all products containing FD&C Yellow No. 5 to identify it on their labels so consumers who are sensitive to the dye can avoid it.

rules.notes: Foods labeled 'gluten-free' must meet the FDA's defined standard for gluten content. Compliance requirements for fermented and hydrolyzed foods bearing the 'gluten-free' claim were established on August 12, 2020.
rules.evidenceQuote: On August 2, 2013, the FDA issued a final rule defining “gluten-free” for food labeling [...] On August 12, 2020, the FDA issued a final rule to establish compliance requirements for fermented and hydrolyzed foods, or foods that contain fermented or hydrolyzed ingredients, bearing the “gluten-free” claim.

rules.notes: Foods or ingredients containing a major food allergen must be specifically labeled with the name of the allergen source.
rules.evidenceQuote: FALCPA requires that foods or ingredients that contain a "major food allergen" be specifically labeled with the name of the allergen source.

rules.notes: The allergen's food source must appear in parentheses following the ingredient name (e.g., 'lecithin (soy)') OR immediately after or next to the ingredient list in a 'contains' statement (e.g., 'Contains wheat, milk, and soy.').
rules.evidenceQuote: The name of the food source of a major food allergen must appear: In parentheses following the name of the ingredient. [...] — OR — Immediately after or next to the list of ingredients in a “contains” statement.

rules.notes: The type of tree nut (e.g., almonds, pecans, walnuts), and the species of fish (e.g., bass, flounder, cod) and Crustacean shellfish (e.g., crab, lobster, shrimp) must be declared.
rules.evidenceQuote: The law also requires that the type of tree nut (for example, almonds, pecans, walnuts), and the species of fish (for example, bass, flounder, cod) and Crustacean shellfish (for example, crab, lobster, shrimp) to be declared.

rules.notes: Various sulfiting agents must be declared on food labels when present in food and the concentration is ≥10 parts per million total sulfites.
rules.evidenceQuote: Various sulfiting agents, including sodium bisulfite, are allowed as food ingredients. But due in part to adverse reactions to them, such as asthma in sensitive individuals, they must be declared on food labels when present in food and the concentration in the food is ≥10 parts per million total sulfites.
